name: admin-notice
summary: Render an admin notice in wp-admin via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a Ready listener that hooks admin_notices and renders a styled notice. Includes a shouldRender() gate for conditional display.
problem: You need to surface a one-time message in wp-admin (setup required, action needed, error encountered) without sprinkling admin_notices hooks throughout the codebase.
appliesWhen: You want a banner-style message at the top of wp-admin pages
Display is conditional on environment, capability, or stored state
The notice can be expressed as static HTML
avoidWhen: You need an inline notice tied to a specific form's submission (use settings_errors instead)
The notice must persist as a long-running task indicator (build a custom UI inside an admin page)
examples: Show a 'configure the plugin' notice when settings are missing
tradeoffs: Renders on every admin page until shouldRender() returns false. For per-user dismissal, store a user_meta flag and check it in shouldRender().
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that hooks admin_notices
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Edit shouldRender() to gate display correctly
Refine the notice HTML — add buttons, links, or richer layout as needed

noticeType.aiHint: One of: success, info, warning, error.

message.example: Acme Billing needs to be configured before it can run.
